I1.label: . Report number of instruc onal faculty members in each category for Fall 2024. Include faculty who are on your
I1.raw_text:
I1. Report number of instruc onal faculty members in each category for Fall 2024. Include faculty who are on your
    ins tu on's payroll on the census date your ins tu on uses for IPEDS/AAUP.

The following defini on of instruc onal faculty is used by the American Associa on of University Professors (AAUP) in
its annual Faculty Compensa on Survey (the part- me defini ons are not used by AAUP). Instruc onal Faculty is de-
fined as those members of the instruc onal-research staﬀ whose major regular assignment is instruc on, including
those with released me for research. Use the direc ons below to determine inclusions and exclusions:

                                                                                      Full- me Part- me
A. Instruc onal faculty in preclinical and clinical medicine, faculty who are not paid Exclude      Include only if they
    (e.g., those who donate their services or are in the military), or research-only                teach one or more non
    faculty, post-doctoral fellows, or pre-doctoral fellows                                         -clinical credit courses
B. Administra ve oﬃcers with tles such as dean of students, librarian, coach, and Exclude           Include if they teach
   the like, even though they may devote part of their me to classroom instruc-                     one or more non-
    on and may have faculty status                                                                  clinical credit courses
C. Other administrators/staﬀ who teach one or more non-clinical credit courses        Exclude       Include
   even though they do not have faculty status
D. Undergraduate or graduate students who assist in the instruc on of courses,        Exclude       Exclude
   but have tles such as teaching assistant, teaching fellow, and the like
E. Faculty on sabba cal or leave with pay                                             Include       Exclude
F. Faculty on leave without pay                                                       Exclude       Exclude
G. Replacement faculty for faculty on sabba cal leave or leave with pay               Exclude       Include

Full- me instruc onal faculty
 Faculty employed on a full- me basis for instruc on (including those with released me for research).

Part- me instruc onal faculty
 Adjuncts and other instructors being paid solely for part- me classroom instruc on. Also includes full- me faculty
 teaching less than two semesters, three quarters, two trimesters, or two four-month sessions. Employees who are not
 considered full- me instruc onal faculty but who teach one or more non-clinical credit courses may be counted as
 part- me faculty.

Minority faculty
 Includes faculty who designate themselves as Black, non-Hispanic; American Indian or Alaska Na ve; Asian, Na ve
Hawaiian or other Pacific Islander, or Hispanic.

Doctorate
 Includes such degrees as Doctor of Philosophy, Doctor of Educa on, Doctor of Juridical Science, and Doctor of Public
 Health in any field such as arts, sciences, educa on, engineering, business, and public administra on. Also includes
 terminal degrees formerly designated as “first professional,” including den stry (DDS or DMD), medicine (MD), op-
 tometry (OD), osteopathic medicine (DO), pharmacy (DPharm or BPharm), podiatric medicine (DPM), veterinary medi-
 cine (DVM), chiroprac c (DC or DCM), or law (JD).

Terminal degree
 The highest degree in a field: example, M. Arch (in architecture) and MFA (master of fine arts).

                                                       29

I3.label: . Undergraduate Class Size
I3.raw_text:
I3. Undergraduate Class Size
In the table below, please use the following defini ons to report informa on about the size of classes and class sec-
  ons oﬀered in the Fall 2024 term.

Class Sec ons
 A class sec on is an organized course oﬀered for credit, iden fied by discipline and number, mee ng at a stated me
 or mes in a classroom or similar se ng, and not a subsec on such as a laboratory or discussion sec on. Undergradu-
 ate class sec ons are defined as any sec ons in which at least one degree-seeking undergraduate student is enrolled
 for credit. Exclude distance learning classes and noncredit classes and individual instruc on such as disserta on or
 thesis research, music instruc on, or one-to-one readings. Exclude students in independent study, co-opera ve pro-
 grams, internships, foreign language taped tutor sessions, prac cums, and all students in on-on-one classes. Each class
 sec on should be counted only once and should not be duplicated because of course catalog cross-lis ngs.

Class Subsec ons
 A class subsec on includes any subsec on of a course, such as a laboratory, recita on, and discussion subsec ons
 that are supplementary in nature and are scheduled to meet separately from the lecture por on of the course. Under-
 graduate subsec ons are defined as any subsec ons of courses in which degree-seeking undergraduate students en-
 rolled for credit. As above, exclude noncredit classes and individual instruc on such as disserta on or thesis research,
 music instruc on, or one-to-one readings. Each class subsec on should be counted only once and should not be dupli-
 cated because of cross-lis ngs.

Using the above defini ons, please report for each of the following class-size intervals the number of class sec ons and
class subsec ons oﬀered in Fall 2024. For example, a lecture class with 800 students who met at another me in 40 sep-
arate labs with 20 students should be counted once in the “100+” column in the class sec on column and 40 mes un-
der the “20-20” column of the class subsec ons table.
